2603.23937.title: Dialogue to Question Generation for Evidence-based Medical Guideline Agent Development
2603.23937.paper_summary: Evidence-based medicine (EBM) is central to high-quality care, but remains difficult to implement in fast-paced primary care settings. Physicians face short consultations, increasing patient loads, and lengthy guideline documents that are impractical to consult in real time. To address this gap, we investigate the feasibility of using large language models (LLMs) as ambient assistants that surface targeted, evidence-based questions during physician-patient encounters. Our study focuses on question generation rather than question answering, with the aim of scaffolding physician reasoning and integrating guideline-based practice into brief consultations. We implemented two prompting strategies, a zero-shot baseline and a multi-stage reasoning variant, using Gemini 2.5 as the backbone model. We evaluated on a benchmark of 80 de-identified transcripts from real clinical encounters, with six experienced physicians contributing over 90 hours of structured review. Results indicate that while general-purpose LLMs are not yet fully reliable, they can produce clinically meaningful and guideline-relevant questions, suggesting significant potential to reduce cognitive burden and make EBM more actionable at the point of care.
2603.23937.paper_summary_zh: 證據基礎醫學（EBM）是高品質護理的核心，但在快速變化的初級護理環境中實施仍然困難。醫生面臨短暫的諮詢時間、日益增加的病人負擔，以及不切實際的長篇指導文件，這些文件在實時查閱時並不方便。為了解決這一問題，我們研究了使用大型語言模型（LLMs）作為環境助手的可行性，這些助手在醫生與病人的互動中提出針對性的、基於證據的問題。我們的研究重點是問題生成而非問題回答，旨在支撐醫生的推理並將基於指導的實踐整合到簡短的諮詢中。我們實施了兩種提示策略，一種是零樣本基線，另一種是多階段推理變體，使用 Gemini 2.5 作為基礎模型。我們在80份來自真實臨床互動的去識別化轉錄本的基準上進行評估，六位經驗豐富的醫生提供了超過90小時的結構化審查。結果顯示，儘管通用型LLMs尚未完全可靠，但它們可以生成臨床上有意義且與指導相關的問題，這表明在減輕認知負擔和使EBM在護理現場更具可行性方面具有顯著潛力。
2603.23937.author: Zongliang Ji et.al.
2603.23937.authors: Zongliang Ji, Ziyang Zhang, Xincheng Tan, Matthew Thompson, Anna Goldenberg, Carl Yang, Rahul G. Krishnan, Fan Zhang
